programs.name: Hand Built Planters
programs.description:
Make a functional planter while exploring a mix
of hand-building techniques in this intermediate-level workshop. You’ll combine
methods like slab building and pinch forms to create a piece that’s both
practical and thoughtfully designed, encouraging problem-solving around
structure, drainage and form. The first two classes focus on building your
planter. In the third session, you’ll learn glazing through a demo, then have
time in the remaining classes to glaze independently. Seasonally inspired and
hands-on, this class is perfect if you’re thinking about plants, repotting or
adding something handmade to your space.

programs.name: Hand Built Planters
programs.description:
Make a functional planter while exploring a mix
of hand-building techniques in this intermediate-level workshop. You’ll combine
methods like slab building and pinch forms to create a piece that’s both
practical and thoughtfully designed, encouraging problem-solving around
structure, drainage and form. The first two classes focus on building your
planter. In the third session, you’ll learn glazing through a demo, then have
time in the remaining classes to glaze independently. Seasonally inspired and
hands-on, this class is perfect if you’re thinking about plants, repotting or
adding something handmade to your space.

programs.name: Ultra Youth Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water and improve your swimming skills. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. There will be a in-person exam on week 10.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Cardio: Rebounder
programs.description: Save your joints from discomfort with this low-impact workout that uses a mini trampoline to support movement. Classes will include both cardio and multi-muscle exercises to help you improve your balance, coordination and endurance.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Gardening
programs.description: This is a great introduction to the garden. Learn how to take care of plants and what makes them grow with hands-on practice and fun activities.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 2
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop front and back floats and staying afloat in the water. Learn front and back glide and going underwater. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 1.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Beginner
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on safe landing techniques, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Beginner
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on safe landing techniques, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Stroke Improvement
programs.description: Work on stroke correction and improving technique through drills and progressions focused on front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Participants in this program will learn the basics of knitting and the techniques involved.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Keep your hands moving with knitting. Learn how to hold and use knitting needles, to cast on and off, knit and purl and following a pattern create your very own project.

programs.name: Land, Light & Legacy: Painting in the Group of Seven Style
programs.description: Explore  the natural beauty of Canada through the lens of the iconic Group of Seven. Study  the techniques, composition and brushwork of these early 20th-century Canadian  painters as you experiment with texture, colour and light while creating your  own landscapes in either oil or acrylic. All levels.

programs.name: Landscape Impressionist Painting in Acrylics
programs.description: Explore landscape painting inspired by the Impressionist movement, with a focus on light, colour, atmosphere and expressive brushwork. Working primarily with acrylic paint, you’ll learn from artists like Monet, Renoir and Cassatt while developing your own visual style. The emphasis is on observation, movement and personal interpretation rather than strict realism. No prior painting experience is required.

programs.name: Language: Spanish
programs.description: Sí! This is the perfect introduction to Spanish. programs.name: Language: Spanish - Advanced
programs.description: If you want to take the next step in communicating in Spanish, then consider this advanced activity. You will learn new vocabulary and grammar and get practice with reading and writing at a slightly higher level of difficulty.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Intermediate
programs.description: If you are looking to continue to develop your Spanish language skills, this intermediate-level activity is a great place to start. Building on the basics, you will get exposure to new words and more difficult grammar. You will also get the opportunity to practice your conversational Spanish with your peers.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. As you continue to practice and develop skills, you will work towards performing a routine at the end of the session.

programs.name: Digital Art: Manga Kids (9-13 years)
programs.description: Create your own step-by-step exciting Manga characters while learning the basics of Adobe Illustrator.  Take home a high-resolution print of your character and a USB with your completed work in JPEG to send by e-mail or to print again at home.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cooking: Vegetarian
programs.description: Get creative with great ingredients like fruits, vegetables and grains. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain comfort in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Basics
programs.description: Combining influences of lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip hop and modern dance this activity is the perfect for beginners. Learn basic positions, body awareness and proper technique through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: Use your feet to create the beat with tap dance. Focusing on balance and coordination, you will learn basic movements like shuffle, flap, heel drop, ball tap and brush through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Music: Guitar
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Youth Leadership Program
programs.description: Building tomorrow’s leaders is what this activity is all about. With a focus on the fundamental principles of healthy youth development, you will learn to engage your community, explore your talents, think critically, problem-solve and make decisions. Through collaboration with your instructor, you will also help design activity content to ensure it is relevant and meets the diverse needs, unique skills and talents of those enrolled.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Learn basic skills to transform clothing into custom pieces. You will learn how to do   basic stitches and be able to work toward  repairs to torn clothing,  lengthening, or hems, replacing or adding buttons and more.    Students must bring their own pieces and notions.  Some practice   materials. Other helpful materials needed: straight fabric pins, fabric   scissors, tailor's chalk or wax, tracing paper, binder, lined paper and   stationary supplies to take notes. Some needles and limited number of   Sewing Machines available.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Participants in this program will learn the basics of knitting and the techniques involved.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Keep your hands moving with knitting. Learn how to hold and use knitting needles, to cast on and off, knit and purl and following a pattern create your very own project.

programs.name: Land, Light & Legacy: Painting in the Group of Seven Style
programs.description: Explore  the natural beauty of Canada through the lens of the iconic Group of Seven. Study  the techniques, composition and brushwork of these early 20th-century Canadian  painters as you experiment with texture, colour and light while creating your  own landscapes in either oil or acrylic. All levels.

programs.name: Landscape Impressionist Painting in Acrylics
programs.description: Explore landscape painting inspired by the Impressionist movement, with a focus on light, colour, atmosphere and expressive brushwork. Working primarily with acrylic paint, you’ll learn from artists like Monet, Renoir and Cassatt while developing your own visual style. The emphasis is on observation, movement and personal interpretation rather than strict realism. No prior painting experience is required.

programs.name: Language: Spanish
programs.description: Sí! This is the perfect introduction to Spanish. With a focus on learning the language and communicating while travelling, this activity will use theme-based lesson plans on topics like food, weather, transportation and more to provide you with a sense of the Spanish language in different contexts.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Advanced
programs.description: If you want to take the next step in communicating in Spanish, then consider this advanced activity. You will learn new vocabulary and grammar and get practice with reading and writing at a slightly higher level of difficulty.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Intermediate
programs.description: If you are looking to continue to develop your Spanish language skills, this intermediate-level activity is a great place to start. Building on the basics, you will get exposure to new words and more difficult grammar. You will also get the opportunity to practice your conversational Spanish with your peers.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Intermediate
programs.description: If you are looking to continue to develop your Spanish language skills, this intermediate-level activity is a great place to start. Building on the basics, you will get exposure to new words and more difficult grammar. You will also get the opportunity to practice your conversational Spanish with your peers.

programs.name: Learn to Sew: Quilted Placemat
programs.description: Quilt your own quilted projects. Using our sewing machines, you'll create a set of coordinating placemats. whether you're new to sewing or looking to try quilting for the first time, this hands-on course will guide you through the essentials and set you up for larger quilts. Material fee of $15 (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography. As you continue to practice and develop skills, you will work towards performing a routine at the end of the session.

programs.name: Digital Art: Manga Kids (9-13 years)
programs.description: Create your own step-by-step exciting Manga characters while learning the basics of Adobe Illustrator.  Take home a high-resolution print of your character and a USB with your completed work in JPEG to send by e-mail or to print again at home.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cooking: Vegetarian
programs.description: Get creative with great ingredients like fruits, vegetables and grains. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain comfort in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Basics
programs.description: Combining influences of lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip hop and modern dance this activity is the perfect for beginners. Learn basic positions, body awareness and proper technique through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: Use your feet to create the beat with tap dance. Focusing on balance and coordination, you will learn basic movements like shuffle, flap, heel drop, ball tap and brush through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Jewellery Making
programs.description: Jewellery enthusiasts will love this activity. Here you will use beads, jewellery findings and wire to make a variety of projects. You will also learn how to use the tools of the trade to design your own masterpieces.

programs.name: Music: Guitar
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Music: Piano - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the piano with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Youth Leadership Program
programs.description: Building tomorrow’s leaders is what this activity is all about. With a focus on the fundamental principles of healthy youth development, you will learn to engage your community, explore your talents, think critically, problem-solve and make decisions. Through collaboration with your instructor, you will also help design activity content to ensure it is relevant and meets the diverse needs, unique skills and talents of those enrolled.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Learn basic skills to transform clothing into custom pieces. You will learn how to do   basic stitches and be able to work toward  repairs to torn clothing,  lengthening, or hems, replacing or adding buttons and more.    Students must bring their own pieces and notions.  Some practice   materials. Other helpful materials needed: straight fabric pins, fabric   scissors, tailor's chalk or wax, tracing paper, binder, lined paper and   stationary supplies to take notes. Some needles and limited number of   Sewing Machines available.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Participants in this program will learn the basics of knitting and the techniques involved.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Keep your hands moving with knitting. Learn how to hold and use knitting needles, to cast on and off, knit and purl and following a pattern create your very own project.

programs.name: Land, Light & Legacy: Painting in the Group of Seven Style
programs.description: Explore  the natural beauty of Canada through the lens of the iconic Group of Seven. Study  the techniques, composition and brushwork of these early 20th-century Canadian  painters as you experiment with texture, colour and light while creating your  own landscapes in either oil or acrylic. All levels.

programs.name: Landscape Impressionist Painting in Acrylics
programs.description: Explore landscape painting inspired by the Impressionist movement, with a focus on light, colour, atmosphere and expressive brushwork. Working primarily with acrylic paint, you’ll learn from artists like Monet, Renoir and Cassatt while developing your own visual style. The emphasis is on observation, movement and personal interpretation rather than strict realism. No prior painting experience is required.

programs.name: Language: Spanish
programs.description: Sí! This is the perfect introduction to Spanish. With a focus on learning the language and communicating while travelling, this activity will use theme-based lesson plans on topics like food, weather, transportation and more to provide you with a sense of the Spanish language in different contexts.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Advanced
programs.description: If you want to take the next step in communicating in Spanish, then consider this advanced activity. You will learn new vocabulary and grammar and get practice with reading and writing at a slightly higher level of difficulty.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Intermediate
programs.description: If you are looking to continue to develop your Spanish language skills, this intermediate-level activity is a great place to start. Building on the basics, you will get exposure to new words and more difficult grammar. You will also get the opportunity to practice your conversational Spanish with your peers.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Intermediate
programs.description: If you are looking to continue to develop your Spanish language skills, this intermediate-level activity is a great place to start. Building on the basics, you will get exposure to new words and more difficult grammar. You will also get the opportunity to practice your conversational Spanish with your peers.

programs.name: Learn to Sew: Quilted Placemat
programs.description: Quilt your own quilted projects. Using our sewing machines, you'll create a set of coordinating placemats. whether you're new to sewing or looking to try quilting for the first time, this hands-on course will guide you through the essentials and set you up for larger quilts. Material fee of $15 (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

Working primarily with acrylic paint, you’ll learn from artists like Monet, Renoir and Cassatt while developing your own visual style. The emphasis is on observation, movement and personal interpretation rather than strict realism. No prior painting experience is required.

programs.name: Language: Spanish
programs.description: Sí! This is the perfect introduction to Spanish. With a focus on learning the language and communicating while travelling, this activity will use theme-based lesson plans on topics like food, weather, transportation and more to provide you with a sense of the Spanish language in different contexts.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Advanced
programs.description: If you want to take the next step in communicating in Spanish, then consider this advanced activity. You will learn new vocabulary and grammar and get practice with reading and writing at a slightly higher level of difficulty.

programs.name: Language: Spanish - Intermediate
programs.description: If you are looking to continue to develop your Spanish language skills, this intermediate-level activity is a great place to start. Building on the basics, you will get exposure to new words and more difficult grammar. You will also get the opportunity to practice your conversational Spanish with your peers.

programs.name: Learn to Sew: Quilted Placemat
programs.description: Quilt your own quilted projects. Using our sewing machines, you'll create a set of coordinating placemats. whether you're new to sewing or looking to try quilting for the first time, this hands-on course will guide you through the essentials and set you up for larger quilts. Material fee of $15 (cash only), payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.
